encounter.reason: Follow-up, chronic disease management, fall prevention
encounter.location: DeKalb Primary Care, 2701 North Decatur Road, Suite 300, Decatur, GA 30033

screening.name: timed up and go (TUG) test
screening.interpretation: mildly increased for age, within acceptable range

screening.name: dressing self-care ability
screening.note: needs help with buttons and shoe-tying

screening.name: medication management ability
screening.note: caregiver fills weekly pill organizer and supervises administration

screening.name: feeding self-care ability
screening.note: can feed self; does not cook

screening.name: ambulation ability
screening.note: steady gait, no assistive device required

screening.name: supervision needs
screening.value: cannot be left alone for more than a few hours

conditions.name: history of mechanical fall
conditions.note: Reassessed at this visit. Low risk for recurrence with home modifications in place. No orthostatic hypotension. TUG 14 seconds.

conditions.name: gradual weight loss
conditions.note: Weight 186 lbs, down from 190 lbs five months ago and 198 lbs over past decade. BMI 26.7. Monitor nutritional status in setting of Alzheimer's disease.

conditions.name: depressive symptoms
conditions.note: PHQ-2 improved to 2/6, below threshold. Previously 3/6 at November 2024 visit.

medications.name: Namzaric (donepezil 10 mg / memantine 28 mg extended-release) oral capsule
medications.sig: one capsule by mouth once daily at bedtime

medications.name: lisinopril 20 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: one tablet by mouth once daily

medications.name: amlodipine 5 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: one tablet by mouth once daily

medications.name: metformin 500 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: one tablet by mouth twice daily

medications.name: atorvastatin 20 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: one tablet by mouth once daily at bedtime

document_references.description: Follow-up office visit note for 78-year-old male with essential hypertension, type 2 diabetes, hyperlipidemia, moderate Alzheimer's disease, and history of mechanical fall. Documents post-ED fall follow-up with orthostatic blood pressure testing and Timed Up and Go assessment, detailed functional status assessment including ADL capabilities, chronic disease management review, fall prevention counseling, nutritional support discussion, and lab orders for hemoglobin A1c, CMP, lipid panel, CBC, vitamin D, and urinalysis with microalbumin.

diagnostic_reports.description: Hemoglobin A1c ordered for interval diabetes monitoring. Last A1c was 6.9% in January 2025. Results pending.
CMP ordered for routine chronic disease monitoring including renal function and electrolytes. Results pending.
Fasting lipid panel ordered for interval hyperlipidemia reassessment on atorvastatin therapy. Results pending.
CBC ordered for routine monitoring. Results pending.
Vitamin D 25-hydroxy ordered to assess vitamin D status, particularly in context of fall history and gradual weight loss. Results pending.
Urinalysis with microalbumin/creatinine ratio ordered for diabetic nephropathy screening. Results pending.
